mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate increases the risk-free discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, reducing present value and leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between FEDFUNDS changes and 20-day forward returns is not negative and statistically significant (p > 0.05) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises more than 0.3 percentage points over three months, corporate revenue growth expectations collapse and earnings multiples contract, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months during any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in retail sales and industrial production indicates a broad demand-supply slowdown that compresses corporate profit margins and triggers de-risking, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint z-score combination of RSAFS and INDPRO does not produce a Rank IC below -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, elevated inflation readings force the Federal Reserve to accelerate rate hikes, tightening financial conditions and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are not significantly lower than baseline during tightening cycles defined by FEDFUNDS > 0.25% (t-test p > 0.05) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts reflects deteriorating household confidence and reduces construction-related earnings, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative correlation between HOUST 3-month changes and 60-day forward returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Negative real GDP growth indicates a contraction in aggregate demand that undermines top-line revenue growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are non-negative in more than 50% of quarters where GDPC1 quarterly growth is negative over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating nonfarm payroll growth signals a tightening labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate revenue expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month changes in PAYEMS do not exhibit a positive Rank IC > 0.03 with 20-day forward returns over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates improving manufacturing sector health and operating leverage, which drives positive earnings revisions and S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between month-over-month INDPRO changes and 5-day forward returns is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
